title: NFL Analyst Identifies the Vikings Player Under the Most Pressure
description: Minnesota Vikings tight end T. J. Hockenson could be in line to have a big year, and The Athletic’s Alec Lewis seems optimistic about where the tight end could head in 2026.The tight end can produce at a high level that much is clear. Just take the 2025 season for example.
fullText:
Minnesota Vikings tight end T. J. Hockenson could be in line to have a big year, and The Athletic’s Alec Lewis seems optimistic about where the tight end could head in 2026.

The tight end can produce at a high level that much is clear. Just take the 2025 season for example. He played in only 15 games, but recorded 51 receptions on 66 targets, for 438 yards and three touchdowns. It was the lowest total of his career, but he still managed to produce at a high level. He missed the final two games of the season with a shoulder injury.

Now, Lewis is predicting this season will be important with a new contract looming.

Critical season forthcoming. Hockenson’s current contract voids after 2026. He is 29 years old. Proving he can produce like he did pre-ACL tear could net another hefty deal.

The 2025 season saw him record 66 receptions for 438 yards and three touchdowns. The tight end was still trying to find that explosiveness he lost following his late-2023 ACL\MCL tear. It showed in some aspects of his play as well. He had a 5.2 average depth of target, which was one of the lower numbers of his career. In addition to not running deeper routes, he also had four drops on 66 targets. This saw him record a 6% drop rate, which was the highest of his career.

In addition, Hockenson was forced to block more. This was a result of inconsistent quarterback play and poor offensive line play. Overall, it was not a good season for a tight end who was one of the most explosive in the NFL at one time.

For his Vikings career, he has recorded 223 receptions for 2,213 yards and eight touchdowns. His best season came in 2023. He had 95 receptions for 960 yards and six touchdowns. He also had a 74.8 catch percentage. To be fair, he recorded the highest catch percentage of his Vikings career at 77.3 in 2025. However, his production was obviously down.

Now, he is looking to bounce back in a major way. The injuries, in combination with an inconsistent offense, have led him to having a reduced role in some ways. This could be his biggest chance to get a strong season under his belt. If he can stay healthy and show that he can perform with the quarterback under center, then he should be able to land himself that new deal. However, the ACL injury certainly looms large, especially for a player who takes pride in receiving and blocking. How he can move this season, two years removed from injury, is going to be important.

When healthy, Hockenson is a valuable piece of the offense. He showed that explosiveness with the Detroit Lions, and he has the opportunity to show it with the Minnesota Vikings as well. At his peak, he can be a game-changer for any offense. Now, he just has to get back to that. Of course, that is easier said than done, but the Vikings’ offense would undoubtedly benefit from him being healthy, as well as taking on more responsibility in the passing game. This will undoubtedly be a storyline heading into training camp.

title: Insider Sets High Price Tag for Potential Blockbuster Wizards Trade
description: This would be a major deal. The post Insider Sets High Price Tag for Potential Blockbuster Wizards Trade appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
The Washington Wizards have given strong signals that they plan to keep Anthony Davis, and he has reciprocated their intent. However, trade speculation continues to swirl around Davis, who could be the key to one team’s goal of landing LeBron James.

That sets the stage for a potentially lucrative opportunity for the Wizards.

Of course, such an opportunity, in all likelihood, would mean the Wizards would be less competitive in the East this coming season than they are setting out to be.

ESPN’s Shams Charania spoke candidly about the Golden State Warriors’ pursuit of Davis, noting that it would take a hefty haul to pry him loose from the Wizards.

The Warriors conversation, that was definitely alive right before free agency started. That’s why Draymond Green opted out. He opted out of $27.7 million. That surprised the Warriors. That surprised everyone around the league. But that was done to give the Warriors the flexibility to try to get LeBron James and Anthony Davis. Those conversations for AD have gone nowhere for the Warriors, from my understanding,” Charania said on the “Pat McAfee Show” on July 23.

“I don’t think that’s going to be a conversation, because it’s at a point now where if the Warriors were to get in that ballgame, they would have to give up multiple first-round picks, multiple draft picks, multiple swaps, all this other stuff.”

Charania noted some teams would argue Davis is worth it if that also lands James.

“If you’re the Warriors, that’s the give and take that you have there,” he said. “The domino effect, that was in play before free agency started. Now that we’re 20 days in, there’s nothing left.”

Wizards general manager Will Dawkins has set the organization up in an interesting position, with an intriguing mix of veterans like Davis and Trae Young and several recent first-round picks, including No. 1 selection in 2026 AJ Dybantsa and 2025 No. 2 pick Alex Sarr.

title: Former Los Angeles Lakers star named most overrated player in NBA
description: A former Los Angeles Lakers star has been named the NBA's most overrated player.  Davis, 33, was recently given the distinction of being the league's most overrated player by Zach Buckley of Bleacher Report, who pointed to Davis' inconsistent availability and declining production as reasons why he topped the list.
fullText:
Former Los Angeles Lakers star named most overrated player in NBA originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

A former Los Angeles Lakers star has been named the NBA's most overrated player.

That player is Washington Wizards forward Anthony Davis, who was one of the best players in the league at one point, but has been hampered by injury issues as of late.

Davis, 33, was recently given the distinction of being the league's most overrated player by Zach Buckley of Bleacher Report, who pointed to Davis' inconsistent availability and declining production as reasons why he topped the list.

"If guaranteed a clean bill of health, would Anthony Davis be the most impactful player on this list? Maybe. But that's a moot point when he can be guaranteed nothing of the sort," Buckley wrote.

". . . Things that used to be automatic with AD are, at best, major question marks. He'll have flashes of his old dominant days and they might get covered similarly since he's such a notable name, but when his employer is merely hoping to get 50 games from him at this point (a number he missed in three of the past six seasons), things have clearly changed."

Davis will have an opportunity to prove Buckley, and other doubters, wrong this upcoming season, which will be his first full season as a member of the Washington Wizards.

title: Havlicek Stole the Pod: What should we expect from the Boston Celtics rookies next season?
description: They have competition ahead of them and limitations to go with some surprising developments that were on display in summer league. What can we expect?
fullText:
Las Vegas Summer League is firmly behind the Sin City Celtics, their identity now starting to morph toward being the Boston Celtics ball club we will see on the court for the NBA's 2026-27 regular season. Training camp and preseason are still there for the deeper rotation players to earn themselves a role, and the Celtics incoming class of rookies and undrafted players will also be fighting to assure their place with the team as well.

Rookies Chris Cenac Jr. and Dillon Mitchell stand out in particular as potential deep rotation options who could conceivably see the floor with Boston this coming campaign. But they have some serious competition ahead of them and some limitations to their play to go with some surprising developments that were on display in summer league. What can we expect from the Celtics incoming rookies next season, and beyond?

To that end, the hosts of "Havlicek Stole the Pod" caught up with Bryce Simon of Motor City Hoops and the "Game Theory" podcast to project what we expect from Mitchell and Cenac in the season to come as well as their ceilings and floors. They also touch on some of the other hidden gems of the Sin City Celtics, and get caught up on what little news there is to dig into. Check it out!

title: Pundit thinks Lamine Yamal is stronger Ballon d’Or candidate than Bayern Munich’s Harry Kane
description: The FC Bayern star seems to get points docked for being well-rounded and willing to help his teams.
fullText:
William Gallas has effectively ruled Harry Kane out of the Ballon d’Or race following England’s disappointing World Cup campaign.

The Bayern Munich striker remains one of the most prolific goalscorers in world football, but Gallas believes individual numbers alone will not be enough to earn Kane the game’s biggest individual prize. According to the former France international, the World Cup played a major role in changing the conversation,“ Gallas told iPredicta. “Harry Kane didn’t have a great, great World Cup. Even if he scored goals, his World Cup was not the best. So, forget about Harry Kane winning the Ballon d’Or.

“You have to be focused on players like Lamine Yamal, who I think is the number one because he won the World Cup and his performances were good, despite having an injury at the start.”

It is a brutally dismissive assessment of Kane’s chances, but Gallas is highlighting the importance of the biggest stage. Kane may have continued to deliver in front of goal, but the Ballon d’Or is rarely decided by statistics alone.

As a counterpoint, it could also be strongly argued that Yamal was only the third or fourth best player on his own team at the World Cup. For Gallas, though, Yamal has the strongest case after helping Spain win the World Cup while producing the kind of performances that capture global attention. The young Barcelona star has already established himself as one of the most influential players in the world, and his tournament only strengthened his claim.

Kane will likely remain in the conversation because of his extraordinary goal scoring record. However, if the World Cup is the deciding factor, Gallas believes the Bayern Munich striker has already fallen behind the leading contenders.

title: WNBA Spokesman Issues Statement After Sabrina Ionescu Denies Receiving WNBA 3-Point Contest Invite
description: It was hard to imagine that Sabrina Ionescu would not be on the list of contestants for this year’s WNBA All-Star 3-Point Contest. Which is exactly why so many quickly concluded she must have declined the invitation when her name was absent from the announcement.
fullText:
It was hard to imagine that Sabrina Ionescu would not be on the list of contestants for this year’s WNBA All-Star 3-Point Contest. Which is exactly why so many quickly concluded she must have declined the invitation when her name was absent from the announcement.

Ionescu herself, however, has since come out to directly challenge that assumption, stating that she cannot decline an offer she never officially received in the first place. But a WNBA spokesman has clarified the league’s process in such matters via ESPN’s Alexa Philippou.

“Sabrina Ionescu said yesterday re: her involvement in the 3-point contest, ‘I can’t decline an offer I never officially got invited to,'” Philippou posted on her X account on Thursday. “From a WNBA spokesperson: ‘Our general process is to reach out to agents to determine a player’s interest, and some accept and some decline.'”

“Seems notable that Sabrina said she never got ‘officially invited‘ while the league said it generally reaches out ‘to determine a player’s interest.’ Have to imagine there was a dialogue of some sorts between the camps.”

Philippou’s post suggests that both Sabrina Ionescu‘s and the spokesman’s statements could simultaneously be accurate. As Ionescu said, she never received a formal invitation directly. And based on the spokesman’s explanation, it appears the WNBA never issued any official invite to begin with. Instead, they likely informally contacted her agent to gauge her interest before they formally initiated any process.

To a large extent, Lynx guard Kayla McBride’s own account seems to further authenticate the spokesman’s process description. McBride, who also does not feature among the contestants, revealed she too only received what amounted to a half-hearted invitation, communicated informally through a text from her agent rather than any kind of formal outreach.

Both McBride and Ionescu have history with the three-point contest. McBride’s most notable run came during the 2019 competition, where she finished as the runner-up behind Shekinna Stricklen of the Connecticut Sun.

Ionescu’s résumé in the competition, however, is in an entirely different category. She is a two-time WNBA 3-Point Contest champion, winning both times she has participated. She also holds the all-time basketball record for the most points scored in a single round across both the WNBA and NBA competitions combined. And she famously took on Stephen Curry in the NBA’s version of the contest, losing only narrowly in one of the most memorable moments the event has ever produced.

Given all of that, it is no surprise that fans were eager to see her back in this year’s competition. And why many appear visibly disappointed when she was not included. But regardless of how or whether she was invited, Ionescu has made clear that she is genuinely excited for the contest. She, in fact, looks forward to seeing who comes out on top.

title: Beatdown roundtable: favorite almost-signing in Ravens history
description: Baltimore Beatdown’s staff offers their favorite almost-signing in Ravens franchise history.
fullText:
Baltimore Beatdown is all about Almost Ravens this week! In addition to our reader-driven bracket narrowing down the biggest near-acquisitions in team history, our staff will also be providing their opinions on their favorite moves the Ravens almost pulled off. Today, we’re focusing on trades:

The Ravens’ near-signing of future Hall of Fame linebacker Bobby Wagner in 2022 is a really interesting one. Wagner himself had a solid year in Los Angeles with another second-team All-Pro berth and may have stayed in Baltimore for a few years. And the Ravens likely would not have traded for Roquan Smith during the regular season and signed him to a long-term extension that has not aged well. Smith has been great in Baltimore, but it’s fair to wonder if a more moderate price for Wagner plus the second-round pick the Ravens sent to Chicago would have been a better allocation of resources. – Nikhil Mehta

My favorite player the Ravens almost signed is Darius Slay. While Slay was on the backend of his NFL journey in 2023-24, he was still playing well and likely would have been a great fit in Baltimore. Placing him alongside Marlon Humphrey, Kyle Hamilton and company could have paid dividends. Slay likely would helped sure up the team’s secondary and alleviate some of their issues allowing big passing plays in recent seasons. He profiled as the prototypical veteran who the Ravens sign and get high-level play out of late in their career. – Frank Platko

My personal favorite player the Ravens almost signed was Za’Darius Smith. When the deal with “Z” was announced, I was so thrilled for him to come back as a Raven. Looking back on it now, if Smith’s deal had stayed, with Mike Macdonald as the defensive coordinator in 2022 and 2023, he could have been an anchor for what would become one of the NFL’s most feared defenses while Macdonald was in Baltimore (once Macdonald got through his early kinks in 2022). Instead, everything went haywire. Smith back out of Baltimore’s four-year deal, then signed a three-year deal with Minnesota. He was then traded to Cleveland before the 2023 season, traded to Detroit in the middle of the 2024 season, signed with the Eagles at the start of the 2025 season, then retired after five games. I was devastated when I learned Smith had backed out with the Ravens.. – Zach Canter

While not my favorite player among the bunch, Ryan Grant has to be my personal favorite “almost signing” of the Ravens, purely for the hilarity that ensued. It’s no secret that Baltimore has historically struggled to field quality wide receivers, and this was never more true than the final few years of Joe Flacco’s tenure with the team. Entering the 2018 offseason, the Ravens desperately needed wide receiver help after the Jeremy Maclin signing did not pan out the season prior. Baltimore chose to bring in two veteran free agents ahead of the draft in John Brown and Ryan Grant. Brown, a speedster coming from Arizona, was given a modest one-year deal worth up to $6.5 million with incentives. Grant, on the other hand, was oddly given a four-year, $29 million deal despite totaling just 985 receiving yards in his first four seasons with Washington. Fans were rightfully puzzled by the signing. As with Maxx Crosby though, a failed physical nixed the deal, which ultimately led to the team signing a much more accomplished veteran wide receiver in Michael Crabtree, who had just been released by the Raiders. Grant fizzled out, playing just two more seasons in the NFL, ultimately proving the Ravens right for backing out of the deal due to health concerns, but the decision to give him such a contract in the first place baffles me to this day. – Dustin Cox

title: Colts owner Carlie Irsay-Gordon presenting Adam Vinatieri for Hall of Fame induction
description: Adam Vinatieri asked Colts' owner Carlie Irsay Gordon to present at his Hall of Fame induction ceremony.
fullText:
Former Indianapolis Colts' kicker Adam Vinatieri will be inducted into the Pro Football Hall of Fame on Aug. 8, and principal owner Carlie Irsay-Gordon will be the presenter.

The Colts' social media team captured the moment when Vinatieri asked Irsay-Gordon to present.

"I just wanted to come in and say thank you so much for everything that you and your family have done for me. It's been such a wonderful ride," Vinatieri said. "I can't even express to you how much you guys have all meant to me and I'd be super, super honored if you'd present me."

Vinatieri added, "If you're willing to do it, I would absolutely love it if you would put me into the Hall of Fame."

Mike Chappell of FOX59 posted that had Vinatieri been a first-ballot Hall of Famer, late owner Jim Irsay would have been the presenter.

a special moment for @cirsay from future HOFer @adamvinatieri. pic.twitter.com/GXcpkOaG16

— Indianapolis Colts (@Colts) July 23, 2026

Vinatieri will enter the Hall of Fame as the NFL's all-time leader in career points. He also holds the record for most consecutive field goals made with 44, career field goals made with 599, and most postseason points as well.

Vinatieri spent14 seasons with the Colts, where he made 85.3 percent of his field goal attempts. In the playoffs, Vinatieri made 81.2% of those attempts.

He was a three-time Pro Bowler and a three-time All-Pro in his career. Vinatieri also won four Super Bowls, including one with the Colts in 2006.

Vinatieri will also be inducted into the Colts' Ring of Honor in Week 6 of this upcoming season.

title: Shaquille O'Neal: LeBron James Ending Career With Cavaliers Would Be 'Historical'
description: Shaquille O'Neal thinks it would be cool to see LeBron James finish his NBA career with the Cleveland Cavaliers.
fullText:
NBA legend Shaquille O'Neal believes LeBron James ending his career with the Cleveland Cavaliers would be "historical."

LeBron began his NBA career with the Cavaliers back in 2003. He played for the franchise from 2003-10 before going to the Miami Heat from 2010-14. The Akron native then returned to Cleveland from 2014-18 and helped delivered the team's first title in franchise history in 2016.

A future Hall of Famer, LeBron is currently a free agent for the first time since 2018. He's expected to sign with the Cavaliers, Golden State Warriors, Philadelphia 76ers or Minnesota Timberwolves.

Many people believe LeBron will sign with the Cavaliers. Cleveland assistant general manager Brandon Weems was with LeBron in Ohio a few weekends ago.

Weems is one of LeBron's closest friends.

"Let me talk to you about Brandon Weems," LeBron's agent, Rich Paul of Klutch Sports Group, said on his podcast. "Brandon Weems is basically LeBron's brother. Now, he worked his own way [up], nobody gave him anything. He's earned to be in the front office of the Cavs. But that is a big feather in the cap [for Cleveland], because of Brandon Weems."

It would certainly be cool to see LeBron end his career in Cleveland. A starting lineup of James Harden, Donovan Mitchell, LeBron, Evan Mobley and Jarrett Allen could compete for the championship next season.

The Cavaliers made the conference finals last season, losing to the New York Knicks in four quick games. Adding LeBron could make Cleveland title favorites next season.

If Harden signs for $30 million and if Cleveland can deal Max Strus and take back no salary in return, the Cavaliers would be able to give LeBron the mid-level exception.

For the Los Angeles Lakers last season, LeBron averaged 20.9 points, 6.1 rebounds and 7.2 assists per game. He's still an elite offensive player despite being the oldest active player in the NBA.

It will be interesting to see where LeBron is playing next season. Paul recently said on his podcast that LeBron will not be "rushed" into making a decision.

"We're not going to be rushed," Paul said on the latest podcast episode of "Game Over" with Max Kellerman and Rich Paul, released Monday evening. "We're not going to be rushed. It's his choice to make and when he makes the choice, he'll make it. ... I saw Adam in New York and we discussed it. And like I said, it's still his choice. So if he doesn't really know, you can't rush it. You can't rush it."

title: Area trio ready to compete at Legion state tournament
description: This year’s Mississippi American Senior Legion State Tournament will have heavy area representation. Three of the four teams involved – the Tupelo 49ers, Baldwyn Blue Aces and Pontotoc Red Sox – hail from the northeast and have played one another nine times in the regular season. The Brandon Black Sox round out the four-team field. Pontotoc beat Tupelo in the finals last season, but the Red ...
fullText:
This year’s Mississippi American Senior Legion State Tournament will have heavy area representation.

Three of the four teams involved – the Tupelo 49ers, Baldwyn Blue Aces and Pontotoc Red Sox – hail from the northeast and have played one another nine times in the regular season. The Brandon Black Sox round out the four-team field.

Pontotoc beat Tupelo in the finals last season, but the Red Sox will need to defend their title without some key pitchers: Connor Yarbrough, Miller Finn and Caden Farmer.

“Those were kind of my top three pitching staff guys that I had,” Red Sox coach Scott Cummings said. “So it's kind of changed the dynamic.”

Many of the Niners on last year’s team still have the taste of that loss stuck in their mouths. The team has been on a mission ever since and, in the eyes of coach Russell Bunch, is playing its best ball at the right time.

Pitchers like Aidan Strong and Brody Wilson have Bunch confident.

“We're on a mission this year to right that wrong, in our eyes,” Bunch said. “But we're going to have to play some really good ball, because I figured Pontotoc, Baldwyn and Brandon will all throw a pretty good punch this weekend.”

Shawn Hairald has been coaching the Blue Aces for over 30 years, and the chemistry of this team rivals any of his previous groups. Hitters like Jase Pee and pitchers like John Greyson Pharr lead the charge.

“If we can pitch and play defense the way we're capable of, we're perfectly fine,” Hairald said. “If we're deficient in an area, it's going to be stringing hits together, because we've got a few that do that well, and then if we could get the others to jump on board, but that's where we're deficient as a team.”

All games will be played at The Plex on the campus of Northeast Mississippi Community College in Booneville. Pontotoc faces Baldwyn to start the tournament on Friday at 4:30 p.m., with Tupelo facing Brandon at 7 p.m.

A champion of the double-elimination tournament will be crowned on Sunday. That champion will advance to the Mid-South Regional tournament in Washington, Missouri, bringing it one step closer to the American Legion World Series in Shelby, North Carolina.

title: NBA Free Agency: More Jalen Duren Intel From Summer League
description: The Detroit Pistons are still working on a new deal for All-Star center Jalen Duren, but it is clear that a sign-and-trade will not be in store this summer
fullText:
Over the last several weeks, the basketball world has converged in Las Vegas for the Summer League. The Detroit Pistons had both of their draft picks in the lineup for each game, and essentially the whole team was there to support the Summer League squad even though they were only able to produce one victory (and four losses) in the series of exhibition games.

The word essentially was used in describing the Pistons’ team-wide support because 22-year old All-Star center Jalen Duren was notably absent from that contingent, but that is no surprise considering he’s still embroiled in contract negotiations with the club.

So far, there has not been an overwhelming amount of progress on either side. Duren would like a significant raise that is at least in the ballpark of a max contract, but the Pistons would feel more comfortable with a more team-friendly deal that allows for financial flexibility moving forward, which will be key since Ausar Thompson will be up next for a contract extension in due time.

In order to help understand that fluid situation, Spotrac’s Keith Smith spoke with multiple people within the Pistons organization, and he learned just where Detroit stands at the moment amid the important contract discussions being held with Duren and his representation.

“We very clearly want Jalen back with us for next year and beyond,” Smith’s front office source said. “If we didn’t, we could have easily done several different sign-and-trade deals. But the numbers have to be right. You saw what’s happened with Boston and OKC and New York. We don’t want to have to shed talent around Cade [Cunningham] and Jalen a year or two from now.”

Based on that statement, Pistons President of Basketball Operations Trajan Langdon is operating with the understanding that he will need to work out a contract extension with Thompson sooner rather than later, and he’d prefer not to handcuff Detroit before those discussions even begin. Right now, the Pistons have more than enough money to allocate for Duren’s upcoming deal, but they want to remain in a position to offer Thompson what he’s worth at the same time. But in any event, the Pistons have zero disillusions over the value that Duren adds to the team, so it is overwhelmingly likely that the two sides will come to an agreement in the near future.

“JD is a monster,” Smith was told by a Detroit assistant coach. “I don’t think any big man in the league has improved as much as JD has in his first four years in the league. Go look at his rookie year. He didn’t know what to do on defense, so he didn’t do much of anything. Last two years, he’s defending at a high level. He reads the floor well on offense. He’s super athletic. I hope everyone can be reasonable and we have him forever in Detroit.”

As a big man who averaged 19.5 points and 10.5 rebounds last season en route to an All-NBA Third Team selection, Duren is still adding to his skill set. That much was clear since Duren was a markedly improved shooter from the free throw line during the 2025-26 campaign, and he ended up finishing in second place for the Most Improved Player award as a result of his hard work.

Now, it’s the Pistons’ job to make sure that Duren continues working hard in the Motor City as opposed to any other location across the league.

title: Bowling: Pluhowsky and Via return to action with Team USA
description: The top of the standings is always the goal for Shannon Pluhowsky, but the top of the podium is unforgettable when she is wearing the red, white and blue. “When you’re wearing USA across your back it’s very special because so few people get to do that,” Pluhowsky said. “Even now when I hear the National Anthem, my mind will wander back to that moment on top of the podium when it played.” The ...
fullText:
The top of the standings is always the goal for Shannon Pluhowsky, but the top of the podium is unforgettable when she is wearing the red, white and blue.

“When you’re wearing USA across your back it’s very special because so few people get to do that,” Pluhowsky said. “Even now when I hear the National Anthem, my mind will wander back to that moment on top of the podium when it played.”

The Dayton area bowler will get another chance to hear the anthem as she was recently selected by the United States Bowling Congress National Selection Committee to compete for Team USA at the 2026 International Bowling Federation World Cup, from Oct. 12-21 at Max Munich Bowling in Germany. It will be Pluhowsky’s 26th year competing with Team USA.

“I was 18 when I first traveled internationally with the team,” she said. “I was a freshman at Nebraska and basically moved into the dorms and left for Japan.”

Four women and four men will represent the United States as Pluhowsky, now 43, will be joined by fellow Ohioan Jillian Martin, Illinois resident Gianna Brandolino and Jordan Snodgrass, of Michigan.

The men’s team also has Miami Valley ties as Chris Via, of Springfield, will compete with Andrew Anderson of Michigan, Texas native Ryan Barnes and Packy Hanrahan of Kansas. The eight Team USA bowlers have collected nearly 140 combined medals – Pluhowsky has tallied 50 medals in Team USA competition including four golds in Hong Kong in 2011 alone.

Via, 34, will be making his eighth Team USA appearance.

“When you are competing for your country, you feel like you have a little more to give,” he said.

The men’s team also has a bit of unfinished business after a ninth-place finish at the 2025 International Bowling Federation World Championships in Hong Kong in December. The women’s team won the team title and were the overall women’s federation champions.

“We want to be the best in the world, so leaving empty handed was definitely disappointing,” Via said. “But I think that will help us moving forward and we can learn from it.”

Junior Team USA: Mission accomplished for 2026 Bellefontaine High School graduate Gracie Hood.

The incoming Mount Mercy University bowler — who finished as runner-up at the U.S. High School Bowling National Championship — recently qualified for Junior Team USA at the 2026 Junior Gold Championships. Six boys and six girls — including the top four U18 male and female bowlers after 26 games of qualifying – were named to the team. Hood was in second place after qualifying.

She expressed her aspirations just weeks earlier after wrapping up the national high school event.

“I want to make Team USA,” Hood said at the time. “That’s my goal.”

title: Dayton Dragons: McCrystal stars in second straight walk-off win
description: The beauty of winning the first-half division title means the Dayton Dragons have Midwest League playoff games to look forward to no matter what happens the rest of the season. But the Dragons aren’t coasting. They will use their final 40 regular-season games just like the two that started this week’s series against Cedar Rapids with a jolt of energy: To prepare for the unknowns of the opening ...
fullText:
The beauty of winning the first-half division title means the Dayton Dragons have Midwest League playoff games to look forward to no matter what happens the rest of the season.

But the Dragons aren’t coasting.

They will use their final 40 regular-season games just like the two that started this week’s series against Cedar Rapids with a jolt of energy: To prepare for the unknowns of the opening best-of-three series against the second-half East Division champion.

On Tuesday, July 21, they came from behind three times to win in 10 innings. On Wednesday, July 22, they rekindled the late-inning magic of the night before.

Tuesday’s walk-off sacrifice fly hero Jacob Friend launched a two-run homer in the eighth inning to tie the score. In the ninth, catcher Ryan McCrystal assumed the role of walk-off hero. He lined a single to right to score Mason Neville from second base for a 6-5 victory.

“It’s good to see those guys coming back,” Dragons manager Julio Morillo said. “They’ve been facing a lot of adversity lately and just keep fighting and putting good at-bats together and making things happen. That’s good to see because that’s what we’ll be facing probably in the playoffs when you cannot give up, especially in a three-game series. You gotta find a way how to come from behind and win.”

In the ninth, Neville pinch hit and drew a leadoff walk. He moved to second on a ground out. The Kernels intentionally walked Peyton Stovall, and McCrystal made them pay.

McCrystal put his catching knowledge to work at the plate to hit a slider — a pitch he had seen a lot of — for the winner. In a 2-1 count the most common pitch would be a fastball, McCrystal said. But …

“There’s no way they were going heater in and not get a strike,” he said. “They were trying to get me to get myself out, so I sat on a slider there on that pitch, and I’m glad I didn’t try and do too much with it.”

Well, 107 mph off the bat was plenty.

McCrystal started the season with the Dragons before moving up to AA Chattanooga to get more playing time. Alfredo Duno, the Reds’ No. 1 prospect, was the starting catcher then. When Duno was promoted to AA at the end of the first half, McCrystal came back to Dayton for the same reason. He is hitting .292 since returning to Dayton.

“You got a guy like Alfredo Duno in front of you it’s not really that surprising,” McCrystal said. “I’ve been lucky enough to play with Duno for two years now, and I love that kid to death, and a lot more than a baseball player. It’s a good thing if they want me to catch a lot more, that they see a lot of value in me like that. God’s got a very specific reason that I’m here, and it’s not my job to try and make it happen. It’s my job to let it happen and develop in front of me and become a better version of who I am.”

As the Dragons prep for the playoffs, some position players are learning to play every day. Friend is one who has grown into an everyday role this season. He joined the Dragons on June 2 and has been a regular, hitting eight home runs.

“It’s fun watching that kid hit,” McCrystal said. “He’s always had so much talent and ability, and last year it just didn’t go his way. It’s hard to hit when you don’t play very often, and it’s always been in there. This is the kind of team he needs to feel free, the kind of guys he needs to have around him where like he can be one of the mainstays in the lineup and show what he can do.”

The Dragons (50-40, 11-13 second half) got a solid four-inning start from J.P. Ortiz, making his fourth start since spending over a month on the injured list. He allowed two runs on five hits and three walks.

Jacob Edwards powered through three innings, allowing three runs, including a two-run homer to Jose Salas in the seventh off the right field foul-pole screen for a 5-2 Kernels lead. Then Stephen Quigley (5-2) pitched the final two innings.

Shortstop Julio Carerras continued to impress in the field and at the plate since joining the team in the second half. He hit a solo homer, his second, in the first inning, and he led off the eighth with a single to set up Friend’s tying homer.

And the table was set for another walk-off and for McCrystal to continue to hit the way he’s being taught by hitting coach Troy Gingrich.

“Troy’s done a great job with me on trying not to hit homers,” McCrystal said. “I don’t have as many as I want, but I know the power’s in there. I’m just really happy that I’m finally ready to be a hitter again and not try and hit all these home runs. Trying to be myself.”

title: Titans Place $48 and $26 Million Starters on PUP List Before Start of Training Camp
description: The Titans have placed two starters on the physically unable to perform list (PUP) before the start of training camp. The post Titans Place $48 and $26 Million Starters on PUP List Before Start of Training Camp appeared first on HEAVY.
fullText:
The Tennessee Titans rookies have begun their first training camp process, with the veterans set to report to the practice field on August 1st.

With just over a week before the official start of camp, the Titans are making their final roster moves before the team gets a first glimpse at the team under head coach Robert Saleh.

Tennessee has recently announced that they have placed two starters on the Physically Unable to Perform (PUP) list.

Alongside, Tennessee recently waived an offensive lineman to add another tackle to the squad.

Titans have placed a starter on IR

New Tennessee Titans head coach Robert Saleh will be without one of the organization’s key mainstays at the start of training camp.

The Titans announced that they have placed safety Amani Hooker on the PUP list, just over a week before the start of training camp.

Hooker, a 2019 NFL Draft selection by the Titans, has been a key contributor to the defense the last few years. In the last 3 seasons, the defensive back started in 43 games while recording 6 interceptions, 24 pass deflections, 237 total tackles and a sack.

Last season wasn’t great for the DB, having a 111.9 pass rating allowed by Pro Football Focus, but now under the tutelage of Saleh. If Amani Hooker can get to 100% before the start of Week 1, the safety could reach new heights with his new HC.

The defender signed a three-year, $48 million contract extension with Tennessee before the start of the 2025 season.

Offensive tackle JC Latham is the other player placed on the PUP list alongside Hooker.

The O-line featured and started 13 games for the Titans last season, receiving a 68 pass block grade and a 71.2 run block grade from PFF.

The publication also noted Latham allowed 8 sacks and 33 pressures last season for the Titans. Tennessee’s O-line tied for the most sacks allowed last season, which can speak to the whole group or coaching staff.

Now under the direction of Brian Daboll, the offensive front 5 could see a better performance. But the new OC will be without a 30-game starter.

Players placed on the PUP List can return to the squad during training camp. Highlighting that these injuries aren’t long-term.

The Titans added a former 2022 NFL draft pick to the roster

Alongside placing two starters on the PUP list, the Tennessee Titans waived and signed tackles.

The Titans waived Ryan Hayes, who originally signed a reserves/future contract with the organization, to make space for Zachary Thomas.

Hayes has played in one game in his career, back in the 2024 season with the Miami Dolphins, but did not feature in the 2025 season. Thomas, on the other hand, has 17 career games played but also didn’t play in the 2025 season.

The moves appear related to JC Latham’s placement on the PUP to keep an O-line rotation around quarterback Cam Ward.

With training camp beginning in just over a week, the Tennessee Titans will need all the help they can get.

title: Nabers Skips PUP List as Giants Eye Fast Return
description: Malik Nabers likely won't start camp on PUP, signaling hope for his knee recovery ahead of the Giants' season opener.
fullText:
Malik Nabers will likely start training camp off the physically unable to perform list. This news brings immediate hope to the New York Giants and their fanbase. A source told ESPN that the wide receiver is not expected to begin camp on PUP. It’s a massive leap forward after a serious knee injury last year. The team announced other players like Roy Robertson-Harris and Thomas Fidone II were placed on PUP. But Nabers is different. He is working hard to get back on the field. The Giants want him ready for the season opener against the Dallas Cowboys.

Nabers suffered a season-ending injury in Week 4 last season. He had a second surgery on his right knee earlier this year to clean out scar tissue (that’s the kind of detail that makes fans nervous). Coach John Harbaugh admitted the injury was “not a simple” one. Despite the difficulty, Nabers spent a good chunk of his summer rehabbing at the Giants’ facility. This was an important step as he navigated the difficult road back. Harbaugh said recently on “The Domonique Foxworth Show” that there was “optimism” with Nabers. The coach also mentioned optimism for running back Cam Skattebo. Skattebo fractured an ankle last season but returned to practice in the spring. Nabers did not return to practice in the spring. Now, the focus is on what he can do during training camp. The team plans to bring him along slowly. Just because he is not on PUP does not mean he will practice immediately. Veterans are scheduled to report Tuesday before the team travels to West Virginia. New York has seven-plus weeks until its opener on “Sunday Night Football”.

This move signals that the Giants are confident in Nabers’ knee. It shows the team believes he can handle some practice time soon. Fans have been worried about his availability for the first few weeks. Being off the PUP list removes a major hurdle. It means he can participate in more activities than if he were on the list. The team still needs to be careful. Harbaugh said they would find out to what degree he can work. But the optimism is real. Nabers is only 22 years old. He has the talent to be a star. If he returns to form, the Giants offense looks much stronger. The team must manage his workload carefully. They cannot rush him back too fast. But starting camp without PUP status is the best possible start. It gives them flexibility to test his limits daily.

Giants fans should watch Nabers’ first practice sessions closely. They will see how much he can do without pain. The team will likely limit his reps at first. But every day he is active is a win. The opener against Dallas is a big stage. Nabers needs to be ready for that moment. If he looks good in camp, the season could be exciting. The team has seven-plus weeks to get him fully ready. Trust the process and watch the progress. This is a positive sign for the future.

title: Kevin Harvick Reflects On Remarkable NASCAR Hall Of Fame Career
description: Kevin Harvick believes his 2003 Brickyard 400 win at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway was the "tentpole" victory that launched him on a NASCAR Hall of Fame career.
fullText:
When Kevin Harvick was a young racer in Bakersfield, California, he dreamed of racing in the Indianapolis 500 and following the path of Bakersfield’s own Rick Mears, a four-time Indy 500 winner. Harvick realized part of his dream by winning at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway, but it was in NASCAR’s Brickyard 400 instead of the Indianapolis 500. Harvick would go on to become an IMS legend with three Brickyard 400 wins including 2003, 2019 and 2020.

Harvick, who on May 19th was named to the latest class in the NASCAR Hall Of Fame and will be inducted next January, believes his first Brickyard 400 win on August 3, 2003, served as the launching pad to his Hall of Fame career.

Harvick had already won three NASCAR Cup Series races in his career when he arrived at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway for the 2003 Brickyard 400. Crew chief Todd Berrier had prepared a fast No. 29 GM Goodwrench Chevrolet at Richard Childress Racing and Harvick put it on the pole at Indianapolis. The following day, Harvick led 33 laps in the 160-lap Brickyard 400, including the final 16 laps to claim the first “Crown Jewel” victory in his NASCAR career.

NASCAR’s Crown Jewels include the Daytona 500, the Coca-Cola 600 at Charlotte Motor Speedway, the Brickyard 400 and the Southern 500 at Darlington Raceway.

Harvick’s previous victories before the big win at IMS in 2003 came with his first win in only his third NASCAR Cup Series race at Atlanta on March 11, 2001. That was an emotional accomplishment because Harvick was thrust into that seat after the great Dale Earnhart was killed in a crash in the last turn of the last lap of the Daytona 500 on February 18, 2001.

He also won at Chicagoland Speedway on July 15, 2001, to score two wins during his rookie season.

Harvick’s second season in 2002 was far more difficult. He had expectations of contending for the championship but instead finished 21st. He did win at Chicagoland Speedway for a second-straight year with a victory on July 14, 2002, but that was a rare highlight in an otherwise disappointing season.

In 2003, Harvick needed a “tentpole” win in a major NASCAR race to put him back on the winning track. It came at the most famous racing facility in the world.

Earlier this year, I had an exclusive interview with Harvick, who plays a major role at FOX Sports as the NASCAR Cup Series telecasts and is the host of two podcasts on FOX including Kevin Harvick’s Happy Hour and SPEED With Harvick and Buxton.

“I think that the Brickyard was really the first one, the first really big win, that got us on the right track,” Harvick told me. “We really didn’t get to be a consistent winner in the Cup car until 2006 and that’s when I learned sometimes it just takes time. There are so many different things that happen off the racetrack, the politics of it all, how to interact with your team and develop the car. All those things just take time because it's at such a different level than you ever get to before that.”

Harvick was a star on the rise in what was then the NASCAR Busch Series (now the O’Reilly Auto Parts Series) and the NASCAR Craftsman Truck Series when team owner Richard Childress gave him the testing duties for Earnhardt’s No. 3 Chevrolet.

“I knew that group and going in it was a lot easier just to go to racetrack test, get out of the car and do that six or seven times a year,” Harvick recalled. “It was a lot different than being in the car every single week.

“It was like being married, right? When you are around somebody every day, you really understand who they are and how they operate and the things that they like and don’t like. There is a process that goes with it.

“I think in 2003 we were we won the Brickyard and were competitive. It’s just it's so hard to get that all those things right to be able to win three to five races a year.”

Harvick’s win at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway in 2003 came when the Brickyard 400 was still near its peak in popularity. The grandstands were packed with over 300,000 fans who would brave the very hot and humid Indiana weather in August.

The crowds at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway were double and triple the size of NASCAR’s other big races.

Plus, the Brickyard 400 was creating its own history at a time when the Indianapolis 500 was in the middle of a split between the Indy Racing League and CART.

“At that particular time, it was treated like the Indy 500, and they would almost purposely not put enough seats in there to make it bigger than the Indy 500 at that time,” Harvick recalled. “One of the moments I remember the most is the pace car ride after the race with Richard Childress and DeLana (Harvick’s wife) riding around the racetrack afterwards, because that’s what they do at Indy.”

It was a moment of clarity and reflection for Harvick because he remembered seeing the greatest names in racing take that same winner’s ride in the pace car after winning the Indianapolis 500 including Rick Mears after each of his four Indy 500 wins.

“To really take that all in was important, because I didn’t really want to race NASCAR because I didn't know anything about it,” Harvick admitted. “Everything that I had been around in my hometown was all about the Mears Family. So, if you wanted to race, you wanted to race Indy car and race in the Indy 500.

“That was as close as you can come to living out your childhood dream, to go to the Brickyard. Everything that you had talked about to that point was wanting to race in the Indy 500.

“To go there and win at the Brickyard was pretty high up on my list.”

From riding around the 2.5-mile Indianapolis Motor Speedway after winning the 2003 Brickyard 400, Harvick’s path would take him to the NASCAR Hall of Fame with some incredible accomplishments.

In a career that ended in 2023, Harvick won 60 NASCAR Cup Series races, including the 2007 Daytona 500, three wins in the Brickyard 400 at the Indianapolis Motor Speedway in 2003, 2019, and 2020, and the 2014 NASCAR Cup Series Championship. Factor in Harvick’s time in NASCAR Craftsman Truck Series and NASCAR O'Reilly Auto Parts Series races, he won a total of 121 NASCAR National Series races, including 47 in O'Reilly Auto Parts Series races and 14 in the NASCAR Craftsman Truck Series.

Harvick's last race was the final race of the 2023 NASCAR Cup Series season at Phoenix Raceway.

Since then, Harvick has joined Mike Joy and former driver and teammate Clint Bowyer in the FOX Sports booth, calling the action in the NASCAR Cup Series telecasts that air on FOX. Harvick’s incredible career has earned him selection to the NASCAR Hall of Fame on May 19.

In addition, Harvick has become active in the podcast world with two highly acclaimed podcasts for FOX Sports, including Kevin Harvick's Happy Hour, presented by NASCAR on Fox, and SPEED with Harvick and Buxton. The second podcast includes IndyCar on FOX lead announcer Will Buxton.

Harvick is also a devoted father, along with his wife DeLana, to their two children, including 14-year-old son Keelan Harvick and 8-year-old daughter Piper. On February 23, 2026, Keelan Harvick signed a long-term deal with Toyota Racing Development to help him learn the skills to one day become a NASCAR Cup Series driver.

“The COVID season (2020) is what started the whole racing thing with Keelan,” Harvick explained. “He was playing baseball and soccer and basketball or whatever sport was happening at the school. For so long, we didn’t really want to get into racing with Keelan, he needed to go do something else. You get burned out on the things that you've done your whole life because it's taken up so much time.

“And then, Keelan decided to race during COVID. And really, we haven’t stopped racing since.”

With Keelan following in his father’s path, Kevin Harvick rediscovered the joy of racing, something that had skipped him by because of the relentless schedule, and often brutal demands that come with racing at NASCAR’s highest levels for more than 25 straight years.

“Since he started racing, it reminded you of why you like racing and that's the people, the places you get to go,” Harvick said. “Karting was a lot of fun for me because that's how I started as a kid and he traveled all over the world doing it. But you remember when you see the parents and the kids and going to these different places and you remember why you love the sport.

“That really helped me get through the end of my career just because of the fact that you were reminded of why you were there on Sunday. It became a job, but it wasn’t a job when you started. You chased that path because you liked what you did. I think a lot of times, you need a reminder of why you like doing what you do. And in our sport, at the highest levels, it can become a job. I think that Keelan racing really helped get me through those last three years. It probably kept me driving longer because of the fact that he was into it."

Harvick is able to share that joy of racing through a depth of knowledge when he is in the FOX booth during a NASCAR telecast, or on his weekly FOX shows and podcasts including Kevin Harvick’s Happy Hour and SPEED With Harvick And Buxton.

“It gives people something positive to think about, the influence that all those moments had on the sport,” Harvick told me. “You realize it a lot better now sitting here at 50, 25 years later than you did at 25.

“I didn’t know that you don’t realize those things until you get a little bit down the road.”

This weekend, NASCAR returns to the Indianapolis Motor Speedway for the Brickyard 400. On Sunday, a NASCAR Cup Series driver will experience the feeling that Harvick first enjoyed 23 years ago when he won the first of his three Brickyard 400s. Just as it did for Harvick in 2003, it could create the foundation of a NASCAR Hall of Fame career.

This article was originally published on Forbes.com

title: Diamondbacks take makeup game with Cardinals as Tyler Locklear stars
description: The Arizona Diamondbacks beat the St. Louis Cardinals by a score of 10-6 at Busch Stadium in St. Louis, on July 23, 2026.
fullText:
Brandon Pfaadt threw six quality innings and Tyler Locklear went 3-for-5 with a homer and three RBIs on Thursday to help the visiting Arizona Diamondbacks earn a 10-6 victory over the St. Louis Cardinals.

The standalone game was a make-up from June 25, when the clubs had their series finale postponed due to weather.

Pfaadt (5-1) won his fifth straight game, allowing two runs on seven hits, walking two and striking out one for the Diamondbacks, who won their third straight and ninth of their last 11. Gabriel Moreno tallied three hits and drove in two runs for Arizona, which posted a five-run eighth inning and had 16 total hits. Adrian Del Castillo and Ildemaro Vargas also finished with three hits apiece.

Michael McGreevy (4-8) threw 6 1/3 innings of four-run, seven-hit ball, striking out three and walking three for St. Louis, which dropped its sixth game in eight tries. Lars Nootbaar drove in three runs for the Cardinals.

Arizona threatened in the opening inning, as Geraldo Perdomo walked and Moreno singled to place runners on the corners with one out. McGreevy escaped, though, getting Max Kepler to ground into an inning-ending double play.

St. Louis began the bottom of the first with JJ Wetherholt's leadoff single before Jordan Walker's RBI double gave the Cardinals a 1-0 lead. Alec Burleson followed with a base hit before Nootbaar's single to center doubled the margin.

Arizona answered in the top of fourth. Kepler drew a leadoff walk before advancing to third on Del Castillo's double. Tim Tawa then drove in the visitors' first run with a sacrifice fly. After McGreevy retired Vargas, Locklear hit his first home run of the season to give the Diamondbacks a 3-2 lead.

Arizona tacked on in the fifth, as Corbin Carroll began the frame with his 12th triple of the year, before scoring on Moreno's sac fly.

After McGreevy retired Ryan Waldshmidt for the first out of the seventh, Justin Bruihl entered in relief.

In the top of the eighth, Moreno doubled and Del Castillo slashed an RBI single off Bruihl, pushing the Arizona lead to 5-2. Matt Svanson then entered for St. Louis, walking Tawa and allowing Vargas' single to load the bases.

The Diamondbacks then blew the game open, as Locklear and Nolan Arenado hit back-to-back run-scoring singles, before Carroll's sac fly extended the lead to 8-2. Moreno continued the parade with an RBI single, his second hit of the inning.

Arenado drove in another run with a two-out single in the ninth.

Ryan Thompson allowed Bryan Torres and Nootbaar's two-run singles in the ninth, but retired Masyn Winn to end the game.

This article originally appeared on Arizona Republic: Diamondbacks take makeup game with Cardinals as Tyler Locklear stars

title: Latest encouraging update on Giants star rehabbing serious injury

fullText:

The Giants did put these players on the PUP list: tight end Thomas Fidone, defensive lineman Sam Roberts and defensive lineman Roy Robertson-Harris. Those are their only PUP list guys.

It sure looks like Giants star wide receiver Malik Nabers has a legit shot at being ready for Week 1.

And ultimately, that’s the goal, as the Giants prepare to report to training camp Tuesday in West Virginia, with their first practice coming a day later.

That’s a good sign for Nabers, though he still has to show he can fully participate in practice before he gets on the game field. Nabers didn’t practice at all in the spring.

He underwent a second right knee surgery this offseason to clean up scar tissue — something that could delay his rehab. There’s also this: Nabers didn’t just get his ACL fixed last season after he tore it. He also underwent a full meniscus repair. That, too, can prolong a player’s rehab.

Nabers got hurt in quarterback Jaxson Dart’s first start — Week 4 against the Chargers, on Sept. 28. He had surgery exactly one month later.

Will Nabers actually be ready for Week 1 this year? That remains unclear.

And even if he does play, how effective/explosive will he be? That’s another major question facing the Giants and Harbaugh, who desperately need Nabers to provide the playmaking foundation for their offense.

title: Joey Logano Dominates North Wilkesboro to Snap His Winless Streak
description: Logano Turned North Wilkesboro Into a One-Car Show — and Flipped His Playoff Fate Every so often a three-time champion spends a night reminding everyone exactly why he has that hardware at home, and Joey Logano picked a 30-year-old itch to scratch. fullText:
Logano Turned North Wilkesboro Into a One-Car Show — and Flipped His Playoff Fate

Every so often a three-time champion spends a night reminding everyone exactly why he has that hardware at home, and Joey Logano picked a 30-year-old itch to scratch.

Logano led 323 laps of Sunday night's Window World 450 at North Wilkesboro Speedway, then beat Denny Hamlin to the stripe by 0.859 seconds — a margin that badly undersells how completely he owned the place. It was the 38th win of his Cup career and the first points-paying Cup race run at the old 0.625-mile bullring in three decades. It also snapped a drought that had stretched back to Texas in May 2025, an eternity for a guy who usually bullies his way into Victory Lane at least once a season.

Watch: A Florida Motorcyclist Fled An FHP Trooper Twice — His Bike Ended The Chase By Catching Fire

Formula E’s Citroën Racing Team Principal Cyril Blais Has Died

The number worth staring at is that lap count. On a flat, single-groove short track like North Wilkesboro, track position is currency and clean air is king. Get out front with the right balance and the field behind you is stuck breathing exhaust and clawing at the same greasy asphalt everyone else is fighting. Logano and crew chief Paul Wolfe clearly unloaded a car that rotated the way he wanted, and on a track this tight that edge compounds lap after lap. Leading nearly three-quarters of a race isn't luck — it's a setup that let him dictate every restart and every long run.

He didn't get to coast, though. Hamlin's No. 11 hung within range through the final stage, and the whole thing pivoted on a green-flag pit cycle. Hamlin blinked first, ducking onto pit road on Lap 359; Logano ran one extra lap, cycled through, and came out roughly a half-second ahead. On a track where passing is a knife fight, that half-second was the ballgame — especially once the final stage reeled off 175 caution-free laps, denying Hamlin any restart to bunch the field or any lucky yellow to reset the gap.

Here's the part that makes this more than a feel-good trophy: the playoff math. Logano rolled in sitting 17th, on the wrong side of the Chase cutline, and left 15th and 49 points clear with five races before the postseason field locks. In a format that hands a single win a near-automatic ticket, a night like this doesn't just add a win light — it drags an entire team back from the brink of watching the playoffs from the couch.

Georgia Driver Charged With Aggravated Assault After Pointing What Looked Like A Gun At A Motorcyclist

Race Weekend Recap: Logano Ends His Drought, Antonelli Rules Spa, and Pajari Stuns in Estonia

There's history soaked into the venue, too. North Wilkesboro fell off the schedule in the mid-'90s and sat essentially frozen until NASCAR revived it for exhibition duty. Logano already won the 2024 All-Star Race here, so the short track and the No. 22 were on speaking terms. Sunday just made it official in the record book that actually counts.

And for anyone who's tracked Logano's career, the timing carries a little poetry. This is a driver still nursing a grudge over 2015, when a Martinsville payback wreck blew up what looked like a title run. He's built a reputation as a streaky, clutch, occasionally polarizing racer who shows up when the stakes climb. A dominant night at a throwback track, capped with what he called "big, smoky doughnuts," is about as on-brand as it gets.

Post-race inspection cleared the car with no issues, so the result stands.

title: UNC baseball pitcher opts to return for senior season
description: The UNC baseball program received good news as Folger Boaz is returning.
fullText:
The North Carolina Tar Heels fell one game short of capturing their first-ever College World Series title. After reaching the final series, they fell to Oklahoma in Game 3, which ended what was a very exciting season.

As we head to the offseason, the Diamond Heels got some good news in terms of a few key players following the MLB Draft. And now, that good news continues.

Per Inside Carolina, pitcher Folger Boaz has opted to return to North Carolina for his senior season rather than pursue a professional baseball career. Scott Forbes and his staff get a boost to their pitching staff with an arm that is experienced.

The left-handed pitcher hails from Pilot Mountain, North Carolina and while he didn't appear on many mock drafts, he was considered an intriguing pitcher. It was also thought he would try to land with a team as an undrafted free agent.

In 2026, Boaz served as UNC's third weekend starter and appeared in 18 games, starting 15 of them. Over 59.1 innings, he had a 6.83 ERA and gave up 86 hits.

Follow us @TarHeelsWire on X (formerly Twitter) and like our page on Facebook to follow ongoing coverage of North Carolina Tar Heels news, notes and opinions.

title: Jaguars roster preview: Dane Jackson adds more experience to CB position
description: With training camp off in the distance, we will be previewing each player on the Jaguars' 90-man roster. Up next is CB Dane Jackson.
fullText:
Between now and training camp, we will be going through the Jacksonville Jaguars' full 90-man roster, previewing each player.

Up next is cornerback Dane Jackson.

Jackson was a late addition to the Jaguars' offseason roster, signed in early June just before minicamp began. Jackson signed a one-year deal worth $1.215 million.

Jackson was a seventh-round pick by Buffalo in 2020. He's played 2,562 defensive snaps, playing in 64 games with 31 starts. Jackson has logged three career interceptions with 30 pass deflections.

Last season, Jackson appeared in two games defensively, playing just four snaps. His special teams experience over his career is fairly minimal, playing 327 snaps over six seasons.

At the top of the cornerback depth chart are Travis Hunter, Montaric Brown, Jourdan Lewis, and Jarrian Jones. That leaves one to two roster spots up for grabs on the final 53-man, depending on whether the Jaguars keep five or six players.

However, there appear to be two clear front-runners for those roster spots -- Christian Braswell and Jabbar Muhammad. Braswell was on the roster last year and saw steady playing time during the second half of the season. Muhammad was a standout performer during offseason programs.

UDFA Preston Hodge is another player who had a strong showing this offseason.

Jackson has experience on his side, but he's going to have to perform well to move up the depth chart. His sporadic special teams contributions may hinder that potential push.

This article originally appeared on Jaguars Wire: Jaguars roster preview: Dane Jackson adds more experience to CB position

title: Myles Garrett's Cleveland home is on the market for $2.1 million
description: Usually, news of a player's home being put on the market is the spark for speculation that he may be looking for a new city in which to play.
fullText:
Usually, news of a player's home being put on the market is the spark for speculation that he may be looking for a new city in which to play. For former Browns defensive end Myles Garrett, the "For Sale" sign has come nearly two months after he was traded to the Rams.

Via Kalan Hooks of ESPN, Garrett's home in Wadsworth, Ohio, is available for $2.1 million.

Sitting on two acres, the house has five bedrooms, six bathrooms, and 4,300 square feet of living space. (There's no mention of whether it comes with his quarterback graveyard.)

The house was built in 2018, a year after Garrett arrived in Ohio as the first overall pick in the draft.

To get a similar home in L.A., Garrett will probably need to move the decimal point one space to the right.

title: Jacoby Brissett practices on first day of Cardinals training camp
description: The Arizona Cardinals opened training camp at State Farm Stadium on Thursday, July 23. Quarterback Jacoby Brissett was a participant.
fullText:
Arizona Cardinals quarterback Jacoby Brissett was present for the first official day of team training camp on Thursday, July 23. That part was expected after Brissett arrived the day before when players were required to report to camp.

Taking part in the first camp practice at State Farm Stadium, however, was not a given. But Brissett was on the field for a pre-practice walk-through, and did some early work in practice after that despite not having been signed to the new contract he seeks.

Jacoby Brissett (7) is indeed practicing today with the Cardinals. So far. pic.twitter.com/moTQolYCB0

— José M. Romero (@RomeroJoseM) July 23, 2026

Brissett is under contract for the 2026 season but seeks a salary more reflective of an NFL starting quarterback, which he was for 12 of 14 games played last season for the Cardinals.

Brissett reported to the team's mandatory minicamp last in June but did not participate in any voluntary team workouts in the offseason.

"I describe it as Practice 1 on July 23rd," Cardinals head coach Mike LaFleur said, earlier saying that contract negotiations have been productive but are still ongoing.

Brissett being on the field — he threw passes to receivers and did drills with the Cardinals' other quarterbacks — seemed to indicate that an agreement on a deal could be closer to getting done.

"He's here, he's under contract, and it's the first day of practice," LaFleur said. "Didn't have him through OTAs or minicamp or anything like that, so right now, just getting him up to speed with our operation, with our cadence, with our formations, our no-huddle package, our pass game, our run game, that kind of stuff. So, not really worried about any of that stuff right now."

Cardinals veteran defensive back Budda Baker took to social media to urge the Cardinals to get a deal done with Brissett earlier in July.

"At the end of the day, I want all of our players to get theirs and ultimately all of our players to be in the building and being able to practice, being able to get better," Baker said. "Because you only get better by practicing. So I didn't know that Jacoby was practicing today, but that's a great thing."

Baker hoped Brissett would work with the No. 1 offense, so he could get a shot at an interception, he said with a smile.

Cardinals offensive lineman Isaiah Adams took part in practice after facing weapons-related charges in Canada earlier in the week.

Adams, a possible starter at guard, wasn't made available to reporters to comment, but his attorney told The Arizona Republic that Adams "is completely innocent in this matter."

"He's doing good," LaFleur said of Adams. "It was good to see him."

The Cardinals opened training camp with four players on the Physically Unable to Perform (PUP) list.

They are linebacker Josh Sweat, offensive lineman Paris Johnson Jr., cornerback Garrett Williams and tight end Tip Reiman.

LaFleur said there isn't anything to be concerned about with Johnson, the Cardinals' left tackle. He called it "maintenance stuff" for Johnson and said he will be back "before you know it."

Sweat is "in a good spot." LaFleur said, and is expected to practice in camp at some point. Baker said Sweat is dealing with previous injuries he's had and is "getting his body right so he can come out and practice."

Johnson and Sweat are dealing with knee injuries at present, LaFleur said.

This article originally appeared on Arizona Republic: Jacoby Brissett practices on first day of Cardinals training camp
